Posting Rules

Posting rules let you define criteria that, when matched, automatically approve and post an invoice without human intervention. A rule can apply to all suppliers (global) or to a specific supplier only. Rules are evaluated in order of most-frequently-matched first, so heavily used rules are checked before rarely-used ones. If no rule matches, the pipeline falls back to pattern-based matching against the supplier's most recently posted invoice.

What posting rules do

When the invoice processing pipeline finishes extracting line items and allocating accounts, it passes the invoice to PostingRuleService. The service evaluates all active rules against the invoice. The first rule that matches has its actions executed. If no explicit rule matches, the service compares the invoice's lines against the supplier's most recently posted invoice and posts automatically if they are sufficiently similar.

Each posting rule has these fields:

FieldNotes
NameA descriptive label shown in the rules list
DescriptionOptional longer explanation of the rule's purpose
SupplierLeave blank to apply to all suppliers; select a supplier to restrict the rule to that supplier's invoices only
ConditionsCriteria that the invoice must meet for the rule to fire (see below)
ActionsWhat happens when the conditions are met (see below)
ActiveRules that are not active are skipped during evaluation
Match countRead-only counter — how many times this rule has been triggered
Last matched atRead-only timestamp of the most recent match

Conditions

Conditions are stored as a JSON object on the rule. All conditions that are present must be satisfied for the rule to match. You can combine multiple conditions on a single rule.

Condition keyTypeMeaning
min_confidencenumber (0–1)The invoice's AI confidence score must be at or above this value. This is in addition to the system-wide autopost_confidence floor.
total_rangeobject with min and/or max keys (numbers)The invoice total must fall within the specified range. Either bound can be omitted.
line_description_containsarray of stringsAt least one invoice line must contain at least one of the listed keywords (case-insensitive substring match).

Example condition JSON for a rule that matches high-confidence invoices up to R5 000 containing the keyword "hosting":

{
  "min_confidence": 0.90,
  "total_range": { "max": 5000 },
  "line_description_contains": ["hosting"]
}

Actions

Actions are stored as a JSON object on the rule. The available action keys are:

Action keyTypeWhat it does
auto_approvebooleanIf true, the invoice is moved through reviewed and approved states automatically before posting
auto_postbooleanIf true, the invoice is posted to the general ledger automatically once it reaches approved status

Example action JSON to fully auto-post a matched invoice:

{
  "auto_approve": true,
  "auto_post": true
}

Auto-posting and confidence

A rule's actions only execute if the invoice first passes the system-wide eligibility check:

  • The invoice must have a supplier assigned
  • All line items must have a GL account assigned
  • The AI confidence score must meet or exceed autopost_confidence (default 0.90, configurable in Settings › Purchasing)

If any of these are not met, no rules are evaluated and the invoice stays in the review queue regardless of what rules exist.

The pattern-based fallback (when no explicit rule matches) compares the invoice against the supplier's most recently posted invoice. It checks that currencies match, line counts are identical, each line matches a previous line by the same GL account code, a description similarity score above description_similarity (default 60, configurable in Settings › Purchasing), and an amount within amount_tolerance percent (default 10.0%).

Managing posting rules

  1. Go to Expenses › Posting Rules.
  2. Click New Posting Rule to create a rule, or click the edit icon on an existing rule to modify it.
  3. Fill in the name, optionally select a supplier, set the conditions and actions JSON, and mark the rule active.
  4. Save.

Rules are evaluated in descending order of match_count (most-matched first). There is no manual ordering; the most frequently triggered rules are always checked first.

Permissions

  • posting-rules-view-any / posting-rules-view — to view the rules list and detail
  • posting-rules-create — to create new rules
  • posting-rules-update — to edit existing rules
  • posting-rules-delete — to delete rules
  • posting-rules-restore / posting-rules-force-delete — to restore or permanently delete soft-deleted rules
